# Pixel Art Visual Style Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** Establish the full pixel art visual foundation — 480×270 viewport, 16×16 TileMapLayer rooms, 16×32 AnimatedSprite2D characters with 4-directional facing, m5x7 pixel font, and fade-to-black room transitions.

**Architecture:** `project.godot` sets the render resolution and texture filter. All 4 area scenes are rebuilt from Polygon2D placeholders to `TileMapLayer` (generated by an `EditorScript` tool). Player and NPCs replace `Polygon2D` bodies with `AnimatedSprite2D`. The player moves into `AreaContainer` so y-sort covers both characters and props. Room transitions wrap the existing `go_to_area()` with an async fade.

**Tech Stack:** Godot 4.6.1 / GDScript, GUT v9.6.0, Kenney Roguelike/RPG Pack (CC0 tiles), top-down character sprites (CC0), m5x7 font by Daniel Linssen (free, itch.io).

## Implementation Status

### ✅ Completed (automated)
- **Task 1** — `project.godot`: 480×270, canvas_items, keep, Nearest filter
- **Task 2** — Asset directories created; assets downloaded and imported
- **Tasks 4+5** — `player.gd`, `npc_base.gd`, `maris/dex/sable.gd` rewritten; `player.tscn`, `npc_base.tscn` updated to `AnimatedSprite2D`; `player_frames.tres`, `npc_frames.tres` generated with full atlas regions
- **Tasks 6+7** — `main.gd` rewritten (y-sort, new entry positions, fade); `main.tscn` updated (Player in AreaContainer, FadeLayer + AnimationPlayer)
- **Task 9** — `tools/generate_rooms.gd` EditorScript written
- **Tasks 11–13** — `dialogue_box.tscn/gd`, `hud.tscn`, `crafting_panel.tscn/gd` updated with m5x7 font + StyleBoxFlat

### 🔲 Remaining (manual editor steps)
- **Task 3** — Populate SpriteFrames in Godot editor *(skeleton .tres files exist)*
- **Task 8** — Create `data/tilesets/station.tres` in Godot editor (atlas, physics layer, wall collision)
- **Task 9 step 2–3** — Set `FLOOR_ATLAS`/`WALL_ATLAS` constants in `tools/generate_rooms.gd`, then run it
- **Task 10** — Verify area scripts compatible with new room layout

### Asset decisions (deviations from plan)
- Character spritesheet: `16x32 Walk-Sheet.png` (CC0, 4 frames per direction, 3 directions in source)
  - Row 1 (y=0) = down, Row 3 (y=64) = right, Row 5 (y=128) = up
  - Left-facing: pre-flipped PNG generated via PIL (`*_left.png`), walk starts at frame 1
  - Idle animations: single frame only
- Tileset: `roguelikeSheet_transparent.png` (Kenney, 968×526, 60×32 tiles at 16×16)

---

## Open questions

None — all decisions resolved in pre-plan design session.

---

## Decisions Reference

| Decision | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Viewport | 480×270, `canvas_items` stretch, `keep` aspect |
| Playfield | 30×16 tiles × 16 px = 480×256 px |
| HUD strip | CanvasLayer on top — does not consume tile rows |
| Room size | **320×180 → 480×256** (breaking change; all positions updated) |
| Door gap | 3 tiles tall at y=6–8 on left/right wall |
| Station layout | Cantina ↔ Engineering ↔ Quarters; DerelictEntrance ↔ Engineering (left wall) |
| Character sprites | 16×32 px, `AnimatedSprite2D`, origin bottom-center |
| Directions | 4 (down/up/left/right) |
| GUT tests | Skipped for `_get_facing()` — verified manually (pre-plan decision) |
| Y-sort | Player in `AreaContainer`; `y_sort_enabled = true` on `AreaContainer` |
| Camera | Follows player, clamped via `limit_*` to 480×256 |
| Collision (body) | `RectangleShape2D(12, 8)` offset `(0, -4)` |
| Collision (interact) | `CircleShape2D(r=20)` offset `(0, -16)` |
| UI font | m5x7 on all 3 UI scenes |
| 9-slice | `dialogue_box.tscn` only |

---

## New Position Reference (480×256 space)

| Symbol | Pixel position | Notes |
|--------|---------------|-------|
| Left wall door center | `Vector2(8, 128)` | 1 tile inside left wall, vertically centered |
| Right wall door center | `Vector2(472, 128)` | 1 tile inside right wall |
| Player spawn from left | `Vector2(32, 128)` | 2 tiles inside left door |
| Player spawn from right | `Vector2(448, 128)` | 2 tiles inside right door |
| Default spawn (center) | `Vector2(240, 128)` | Center of 480×256 room |
| Door shape | `RectangleShape2D(16, 48)` | 1×3 tiles |

## Batch 1 — Project Foundation

### Task 1: Configure `project.godot`

**Files:**
- Modify: `project.godot`

**Depends on:** none
**Parallelizable with:** Task 2

**Step 1: Apply settings in Godot editor**

Open Project → Project Settings and set:

| Section | Key | Value |
|---------|-----|-------|
| Display > Window | Size > Viewport Width | `480` |
| Display > Window | Size > Viewport Height | `270` |
| Display > Window | Stretch > Mode | `canvas_items` |
| Display > Window | Stretch > Aspect | `keep` |
| Rendering > Textures | Canvas Textures > Default Texture Filter | `Nearest` |

**Step 2: Verify in `project.godot`**

The file should now contain:
```ini
[display]
window/size/viewport_width=480
window/size/viewport_height=270
window/stretch/mode="canvas_items"
window/stretch/aspect="keep"

[rendering]
textures/canvas_textures/default_texture_filter=0
```

**Step 3: Run the game and confirm**

```bash
godot
```

Expected: window renders at 480×270 (or scaled up), game is NOT blurry at any window size, existing scenes still open without errors.

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add project.godot
git commit -m "feat: set 480x270 viewport with canvas_items stretch and Nearest texture filter"
```

---

### Task 2: Download and import placeholder assets

**Files:**
- Create: `art/placeholder/tiles/` (Kenney tileset PNGs)
- Create: `art/placeholder/characters/` (character spritesheet PNGs)
- Create: `art/placeholder/fonts/m5x7.ttf`

**Depends on:** none
**Parallelizable with:** Task 1

**Step 1: Download Kenney Roguelike/RPG Pack**

Go to: https://kenney.nl/assets/roguelike-rpg-pack

Download the pack. Extract. Find a PNG atlas that has 16×16 floor and wall tiles (look for `tilemap.png` or similar). Copy it to `art/placeholder/tiles/kenney_tileset.png`.

**Step 2: Download a top-down character spritesheet**

You need a PNG atlas with 16×32 px character frames, 4-directional walk/idle cycles. A suitable free pack:
- https://agusstt.itch.io/top-down-rpg-character-sprite-walk-idle-animation (CC0, 16×32, 4 directions)

If unavailable, any CC0 top-down RPG character pack with 16×32 sprites works. Expected layout:
- Row 0 (y=0): walk_down — 8 frames
- Row 1 (y=32): walk_left — 8 frames
- Row 2 (y=64): walk_right — 8 frames
- Row 3 (y=96): walk_up — 8 frames

Copy one spritesheet per character to:
- `art/placeholder/characters/player.png`
- `art/placeholder/characters/maris.png`
- `art/placeholder/characters/dex.png`
- `art/placeholder/characters/sable.png`

If you only have one spritesheet, copy it 4 times — use `AnimatedSprite2D.modulate` to tint each character differently at runtime.

**Step 3: Download m5x7 font**

Go to: https://managore.itch.io/m5x7

Download. Extract the `.ttf` file. Copy to `art/placeholder/fonts/m5x7.ttf`.

**Step 4: Import into Godot**

Open Godot. In the FileSystem panel, navigate to each new file. Godot auto-imports them.

For `art/placeholder/fonts/m5x7.ttf`:
- Select the file in FileSystem panel
- Open the Import tab
- Set "Antialiasing" → `None`
- Disable "Hinting"
- Click "Reimport"

For each PNG in `art/placeholder/tiles/` and `art/placeholder/characters/`:
- Import tab → Filter → `Nearest`
- Click "Reimport"

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add art/placeholder/
git commit -m "feat: add placeholder CC0 assets — Kenney tiles, character sprites, m5x7 font"
```

## Batch 2 — Character Visuals

### Task 3: Create SpriteFrames resources

**Files:**
- Create: `data/sprites/player_frames.tres`
- Create: `data/sprites/npc_frames.tres` (shared by all 3 NPCs; tinted per character at runtime)

**Depends on:** Task 2
**Parallelizable with:** none (Tasks 4 and 5 both need this output)

**Step 1: Create player SpriteFrames**

In Godot editor, in the FileSystem panel, right-click `data/sprites/` → New Resource → `SpriteFrames`. Save as `player_frames.tres`.

Double-click `player_frames.tres` to open the SpriteFrames editor. Create the following animations (delete the default "default"):

| Animation | FPS | Loop | Frames |
|-----------|-----|------|--------|
| `walk_down` | 8 | true | Row 0 of `art/placeholder/characters/player.png`, frames 0–7 |
| `walk_left` | 8 | true | Row 1, frames 0–7 |
| `walk_right` | 8 | true | Row 2, frames 0–7 |
| `walk_up` | 8 | true | Row 3, frames 0–7 |
| `idle_down` | 4 | true | Row 0, frames 0–1 |
| `idle_left` | 4 | true | Row 1, frames 0–1 |
| `idle_right` | 4 | true | Row 2, frames 0–1 |
| `idle_up` | 4 | true | Row 3, frames 0–1 |

To add frames: click the filmstrip icon → "Add frames from sprite sheet" → select `player.png` → set grid to 16×32 → select the appropriate row's frames.

**Step 2: Create NPC SpriteFrames**

Repeat for `data/sprites/npc_frames.tres`. Use the same structure but source from `art/placeholder/characters/maris.png` (or any one character PNG — NPCs are tinted via `modulate` at runtime).

**Step 3: Verify**

Open both `.tres` files. Confirm 8 animations each, frames populated, no missing textures.

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add data/sprites/
git commit -m "feat: add SpriteFrames resources for player and NPCs"
```

---

### Task 4: Update `player.tscn` and `player.gd`

**Files:**
- Modify: `scenes/characters/player.tscn`
- Modify: `scripts/characters/player.gd`

**Depends on:** Task 3
**Parallelizable with:** Task 5

> **Test gate exception:** `_get_facing()` contains branching logic but GUT tests were explicitly skipped (pre-plan decision — 4 trivial cases, 30s manual verification). Extract as a pure helper anyway for future testability.

**Step 1: Update `player.tscn`**

Open `scenes/characters/player.tscn` in Godot editor.

Remove the `Body` (Polygon2D) node.

Add an `AnimatedSprite2D` node as a direct child of `Player` (CharacterBody2D):
- Name: `AnimatedSprite2D`
- `sprite_frames`: `data/sprites/player_frames.tres`
- `animation`: `idle_down`
- `offset`: `Vector2(0, -16)` — centers the 16×32 sprite above the origin (feet at y=0, head at y=-32)

Update `CollisionShape2D` (body):
- Shape: `RectangleShape2D`
- Shape size: `Vector2(12, 8)`
- Node position: `Vector2(0, -4)`

Update the `InteractionZone/CollisionShape2D`:
- Shape: `CircleShape2D`
- Radius: `20`
- Node position: `Vector2(0, -16)`

The `Camera2D` node stays. Leave its position at `Vector2(0, 0)` — its limits are set in script.

**Step 2: Write `player.gd`**

```gdscript
# scripts/characters/player.gd
extends CharacterBody2D

const SPEED = 80.0
const ROOM_W = 30
const ROOM_H = 16
const TILE_SIZE = 16

@export var attack_damage: int = 1

@onready var interaction_zone: Area2D = $InteractionZone
@onready var sprite: AnimatedSprite2D = $AnimatedSprite2D
@onready var camera: Camera2D = $Camera2D

var _facing: String = "down"

func _ready() -> void:
	add_to_group("player")
	camera.position_smoothing_enabled = false
	camera.limit_left = 0
	camera.limit_top = 0
	camera.limit_right = ROOM_W * TILE_SIZE   # 480
	camera.limit_bottom = ROOM_H * TILE_SIZE  # 256

func _physics_process(_delta: float) -> void:
	var direction = Vector2(
		Input.get_axis("ui_left", "ui_right"),
		Input.get_axis("ui_up", "ui_down")
	).normalized()
	velocity = direction * SPEED
	move_and_slide()
	_update_animation(direction)

func _update_animation(direction: Vector2) -> void:
	if direction != Vector2.ZERO:
		_facing = _get_facing(direction)
		sprite.play("walk_" + _facing)
	else:
		sprite.play("idle_" + _facing)

func _get_facing(direction: Vector2) -> String:
	if abs(direction.x) >= abs(direction.y):
		return "right" if direction.x > 0 else "left"
	return "down" if direction.y > 0 else "up"

func _unhandled_input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if event.is_action_pressed("interact"):
		_try_interact()
	if event.is_action_pressed("attack"):
		_do_attack()

func _try_interact() -> void:
	var bodies = interaction_zone.get_overlapping_bodies()
	var areas = interaction_zone.get_overlapping_areas()
	for body in bodies:
		if body.is_in_group("interactable"):
			body.interact()
			return
	for area in areas:
		if area.is_in_group("interactable"):
			area.interact()
			return

func _do_attack() -> void:
	var hitbox = Area2D.new()
	var shape = CollisionShape2D.new()
	shape.shape = CircleShape2D.new()
	shape.shape.radius = 20.0
	hitbox.add_child(shape)
	hitbox.position = position + Vector2(16, 0)
	get_parent().add_child(hitbox)
	await get_tree().create_timer(0.1).timeout
	for body in hitbox.get_overlapping_bodies():
		if body.has_method("take_damage"):
			body.take_damage(attack_damage)
	hitbox.queue_free()
```

**Step 3: Verify**

Run the scene standalone (`scenes/characters/player.tscn`). Confirm:
- Player sprite visible (not blank)
- Moves in all 4 directions with WASD/arrows
- Sprite switches animation when moving, reverts to idle when stopped
- Sprite faces the correct direction

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add scenes/characters/player.tscn scripts/characters/player.gd
git commit -m "feat: player — AnimatedSprite2D with 4-directional facing, pixel-perfect camera"
```

### Task 5: Update `npc_base.tscn` and `npc_base.gd`

**Files:**
- Modify: `scenes/characters/npc_base.tscn`
- Modify: `scripts/characters/npc_base.gd`

**Depends on:** Task 3
**Parallelizable with:** Task 4

**Step 1: Update `npc_base.tscn`**

Open `scenes/characters/npc_base.tscn`.

Remove the `Body` (Polygon2D) node.

Add `AnimatedSprite2D` as direct child of `NPC`:
- Name: `AnimatedSprite2D`
- `sprite_frames`: `data/sprites/npc_frames.tres`
- `animation`: `idle_down`
- `offset`: `Vector2(0, -16)`

Update `CollisionShape2D` (body):
- Shape: `RectangleShape2D(12, 8)`
- Node position: `Vector2(0, -4)`

Update `InteractionArea/CollisionShape2D`:
- Shape: `CircleShape2D(radius=20)`
- Node position: `Vector2(0, -16)`

**Step 2: Update `npc_base.gd`**

Add the following to the existing script. Add new `@onready` and `_facing` var near the top, update `_physics_process`, add `_get_facing` helper:

```gdscript
# scripts/characters/npc_base.gd
extends CharacterBody2D

@export var npc_id: String = "unknown"
@export var display_name: String = "NPC"
@export var wander_bounds: Rect2 = Rect2(-50, -50, 100, 100)

const WANDER_SPEED = 30.0

@onready var wander_timer: Timer = $WanderTimer
@onready var interaction_area: Area2D = $InteractionArea
@onready var sprite: AnimatedSprite2D = $AnimatedSprite2D

var _wander_target: Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO
var _is_talking: bool = false
var _facing: String = "down"

func _ready() -> void:
	add_to_group("npcs")
	add_to_group("interactable")
	wander_timer.timeout.connect(_pick_wander_target)
	wander_timer.wait_time = randf_range(2.0, 5.0)
	wander_timer.start()
	_pick_wander_target()

func _physics_process(_delta: float) -> void:
	if _is_talking:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		move_and_slide()
		sprite.play("idle_" + _facing)
		return
	var diff = _wander_target - position
	if diff.length() > 4.0:
		velocity = diff.normalized() * WANDER_SPEED
		_facing = _get_facing(velocity)
		sprite.play("walk_" + _facing)
	else:
		velocity = Vector2.ZERO
		sprite.play("idle_" + _facing)
	move_and_slide()

func _get_facing(direction: Vector2) -> String:
	if abs(direction.x) >= abs(direction.y):
		return "right" if direction.x > 0 else "left"
	return "down" if direction.y > 0 else "up"

func _pick_wander_target() -> void:
	_wander_target = position + Vector2(
		randf_range(wander_bounds.position.x, wander_bounds.end.x),
		randf_range(wander_bounds.position.y, wander_bounds.end.y)
	)
	wander_timer.wait_time = randf_range(2.0, 5.0)
	wander_timer.start()

func interact() -> void:
	_is_talking = true
	var tree = get_dialogue_tree()
	DialogueManager.conversation_ended.connect(_on_conversation_ended, CONNECT_ONE_SHOT)
	DialogueManager.start_conversation(tree)
	get_tree().get_first_node_in_group("dialogue_box").show_current_node()

func _on_conversation_ended() -> void:
	_is_talking = false

func get_dialogue_tree() -> String:
	return ""  # Overridden by subclass scripts (maris.gd, dex.gd, sable.gd)
```

**Step 3: Set per-character tint colors**

Open each character script and add a `_ready()` override to tint the sprite (so NPCs look distinct with the same base spritesheet):

`scripts/characters/maris.gd` — add at top of `_ready()`:
```gdscript
func _ready() -> void:
	super._ready()
	$AnimatedSprite2D.modulate = Color(1.0, 0.7, 0.7)   # warm pink tint
```

`scripts/characters/dex.gd` — add:
```gdscript
func _ready() -> void:
	super._ready()
	$AnimatedSprite2D.modulate = Color(0.7, 0.9, 1.0)   # cool blue tint
```

`scripts/characters/sable.gd` — add:
```gdscript
func _ready() -> void:
	super._ready()
	$AnimatedSprite2D.modulate = Color(0.7, 1.0, 0.7)   # green tint
```

**Step 4: Verify**

Run the game. Confirm NPCs render with sprites, wander with facing updates, and show distinct tint colors.

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add scenes/characters/npc_base.tscn scripts/characters/npc_base.gd \
        scripts/characters/maris.gd scripts/characters/dex.gd scripts/characters/sable.gd
git commit -m "feat: NPCs — AnimatedSprite2D with 4-directional facing and per-character tint"
```

## Batch 3 — Main Scene Restructure

### Task 6: Move player into `AreaContainer`, enable Y-sort, update `main.gd`

**Files:**
- Modify: `scenes/main.tscn`
- Modify: `scripts/main.gd`

**Depends on:** Smoketest Checkpoint 2
**Parallelizable with:** none (Task 7 writes the same files)

**Step 1: Update `main.tscn`**

Open `scenes/main.tscn` in the editor.

Move the `Player` node: drag it from being a direct child of `Main` into `AreaContainer`. It should now be at path `Main/AreaContainer/Player`.

Select `AreaContainer` (Node2D). In the Inspector, enable `y_sort_enabled = true`.

**Step 2: Update `scripts/main.gd`**

Replace the entire file:

```gdscript
# scripts/main.gd
extends Node2D

const AREA_SCENES = {
	"cantina": "res://scenes/areas/cantina.tscn",
	"engineering": "res://scenes/areas/engineering.tscn",
	"quarters": "res://scenes/areas/quarters.tscn",
	"derelict_entrance": "res://scenes/areas/derelict_entrance.tscn",
}

const NPC_SPAWN_AREAS = {
	"maris": "cantina",
	"sable": "cantina",
	"dex": "engineering",
}

# Updated for 480×256 room size (30×16 tiles × 16px)
# Left wall door → spawn at x=32; right wall door → spawn at x=448
const AREA_ENTRY_POSITIONS = {
	"cantina": { "engineering": Vector2(448, 128), "default": Vector2(240, 128) },
	"engineering": { "cantina": Vector2(32, 128), "quarters": Vector2(448, 128), "default": Vector2(240, 128) },
	"quarters": { "engineering": Vector2(32, 128), "default": Vector2(240, 128) },
	"derelict_entrance": { "engineering": Vector2(32, 128), "default": Vector2(240, 128) },
}

@onready var area_container: Node2D = $AreaContainer
@onready var player: CharacterBody2D = $AreaContainer/Player
@onready var hud = $HUD
@onready var day_summary = $DaySummary
@onready var crafting_panel = $CraftingPanel
@onready var arc_events = $ArcEvents

var _current_area_id: String = ""
var _current_area: Node = null
var _npc_instances: Dictionary = {}

func _ready() -> void:
	DayManager.day_ended.connect(_on_day_ended)
	DayManager.all_beats_done.connect(_on_all_beats_done)
	_spawn_npcs()
	go_to_area("cantina")

func _spawn_npcs() -> void:
	var npc_scripts = {
		"maris": "res://scripts/characters/maris.gd",
		"sable": "res://scripts/characters/sable.gd",
		"dex": "res://scripts/characters/dex.gd",
	}
	for npc_id in npc_scripts:
		var base = load("res://scenes/characters/npc_base.tscn").instantiate()
		base.set_script(load(npc_scripts[npc_id]))
		area_container.add_child(base)
		_npc_instances[npc_id] = base

func go_to_area(area_id: String) -> void:
	if _current_area_id == area_id:
		return
	var prev = _current_area_id

	if _current_area:
		_current_area.queue_free()
		_current_area = null

	var scene = load(AREA_SCENES[area_id])
	_current_area = scene.instantiate()
	area_container.add_child(_current_area)
	area_container.move_child(_current_area, 0)  # TileMap renders behind player + NPCs
	_current_area_id = area_id

	var positions = AREA_ENTRY_POSITIONS.get(area_id, {})
	player.position = positions.get(prev, positions.get("default", Vector2(240, 128)))

	for npc_id in _npc_instances:
		var npc = _npc_instances[npc_id]
		var spawn_area = NPC_SPAWN_AREAS.get(npc_id, "cantina")
		npc.visible = (spawn_area == area_id)
		if spawn_area == area_id:
			var spawn = _current_area.get_node_or_null("%sSpawn" % npc_id.capitalize())
			if spawn:
				npc.position = spawn.global_position
			if npc.has_method("_pick_wander_target"):
				npc._pick_wander_target()

func open_crafting() -> void:
	crafting_panel.open()

func advance_day() -> void:
	day_summary.show_summary()

func show_hud_message(text: String) -> void:
	hud.show_message(text)

func _on_day_ended(day: int) -> void:
	if day == 7:
		_show_ending()
	else:
		if DayManager.current_day == 7:
			arc_events.trigger_final_choice()

func _on_all_beats_done() -> void:
	show_hud_message("All story beats complete. Rest at your bunk in Quarters.")

func _show_ending() -> void:
	if GameState.get_flag("ending_pragmatic"):
		get_tree().change_scene_to_file("res://scenes/ui/ending_pragmatic.tscn")
	elif GameState.get_flag("ending_hopeful"):
		get_tree().change_scene_to_file("res://scenes/ui/ending_hopeful.tscn")
	else:
		get_tree().change_scene_to_file("res://scenes/ui/ending_deferred.tscn")
```

**Step 3: Verify**

Run the game. Confirm:
- Game launches without errors
- Player appears in Cantina
- NPCs appear and wander
- Player walks behind a prop and renders behind it (y-sort working)

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add scenes/main.tscn scripts/main.gd
git commit -m "refactor: move player into AreaContainer, enable y-sort, update entry positions for 480x256 rooms"
```

---

### Task 7: Add FadeLayer and async fade to `go_to_area()`

**Files:**
- Modify: `scenes/main.tscn`
- Modify: `scripts/main.gd`

**Depends on:** Task 6
**Parallelizable with:** none (writes same files as Task 6)

**Step 1: Add FadeLayer to `main.tscn`**

Open `scenes/main.tscn`. Add a new `CanvasLayer` node as a direct child of `Main`:
- Name: `FadeLayer`
- `layer`: `10` (renders above all game content)

Add a `ColorRect` as child of `FadeLayer`:
- Name: `ColorRect`
- `color`: `Color(0, 0, 0, 1)` (solid black)
- Anchors: full rect (anchor_left=0, anchor_top=0, anchor_right=1, anchor_bottom=1)
- `modulate`: `Color(1, 1, 1, 0)` — starts fully transparent

Add an `AnimationPlayer` as child of `FadeLayer`:
- Name: `AnimationPlayer`

In the AnimationPlayer, create two animations:

**Animation "fade_out"** (duration 0.3s):
- Track: `ColorRect:modulate:a`
- Key at t=0.0: value=`0.0`
- Key at t=0.3: value=`1.0`

**Animation "fade_in"** (duration 0.3s):
- Track: `ColorRect:modulate:a`
- Key at t=0.0: value=`1.0`
- Key at t=0.3: value=`0.0`

**Step 2: Update `go_to_area()` in `scripts/main.gd` to async fade**

Add two `@onready` lines after the existing ones:
```gdscript
@onready var fade_anim: AnimationPlayer = $FadeLayer/AnimationPlayer
```

Replace the `go_to_area()` function:

```gdscript
func go_to_area(area_id: String) -> void:
	if _current_area_id == area_id:
		return
	var prev = _current_area_id

	fade_anim.play("fade_out")
	await fade_anim.animation_finished

	if _current_area:
		_current_area.queue_free()
		_current_area = null

	var scene = load(AREA_SCENES[area_id])
	_current_area = scene.instantiate()
	area_container.add_child(_current_area)
	area_container.move_child(_current_area, 0)
	_current_area_id = area_id

	var positions = AREA_ENTRY_POSITIONS.get(area_id, {})
	player.position = positions.get(prev, positions.get("default", Vector2(240, 128)))

	for npc_id in _npc_instances:
		var npc = _npc_instances[npc_id]
		var spawn_area = NPC_SPAWN_AREAS.get(npc_id, "cantina")
		npc.visible = (spawn_area == area_id)
		if spawn_area == area_id:
			var spawn = _current_area.get_node_or_null("%sSpawn" % npc_id.capitalize())
			if spawn:
				npc.position = spawn.global_position
			if npc.has_method("_pick_wander_target"):
				npc._pick_wander_target()

	fade_anim.play("fade_in")
```

**Step 3: Verify**

Run the game. Walk through a door. Confirm:
- Screen fades to black (~0.3s)
- New room appears
- Screen fades back in (~0.3s)
- Player spawns at the correct door position in the new room

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add scenes/main.tscn scripts/main.gd
git commit -m "feat: fade-to-black room transitions via AnimationPlayer + FadeLayer CanvasLayer"
```

```

```

```bash
godot
```

Expected:
- Walk player behind a prop — player renders behind it (y-sort)
- Walk player through a door trigger — screen fades black, new room appears, fades back in
- Player spawns near the correct wall in the new room

Tell the user: "Can you confirm (1) y-sort works — player disappears behind a prop, and (2) room transitions fade to black and back?"

## Batch 4 — TileMap Rooms

### Task 8: Create `station.tres` TileSet and configure physics layer

**Files:**
- Create: `data/tilesets/station.tres`

**Depends on:** Task 2 (tileset PNG must be imported)
**Parallelizable with:** none (Task 9 depends on this)

**Step 1: Create the TileSet resource**

In the Godot FileSystem panel, right-click `data/tilesets/` → New Resource → `TileSet`. Save as `station.tres`.

**Step 2: Add the Kenney tileset as an atlas source**

Select `station.tres` in the FileSystem. Open the TileSet editor (bottom panel).

In the TileSet editor:
- Click the `+` icon → "Atlas"
- Set `texture` to `art/placeholder/tiles/kenney_tileset.png`
- Set tile size: `16×16`
- Godot will auto-detect tiles from the atlas

**Step 3: Identify floor and wall atlas coordinates**

In the atlas view, note the `Vector2i(column, row)` coordinates for:
- **One floor tile** (e.g. a plain floor/ground tile)
- **One wall tile** (e.g. a solid wall/stone tile with visual presence)

Write these down — you'll use them as `FLOOR_ATLAS` and `WALL_ATLAS` in Task 9.

**Step 4: Add a physics layer**

In the TileSet editor, select the "Physics Layers" section. Click "Add Element". This creates Physics Layer 0.

Set the layer's collision layer and mask to `1` (layer 1, mask 1) — matching the player's and NPC's physics layers.

**Step 5: Add collision shape to wall tiles**

In the atlas view, click your chosen wall tile. In the "Physics" tab on the right:
- Click "Add Polygon"
- Draw a full-tile rectangle (or use the auto-generate button for a 16×16 rect)

Floor tiles: leave Physics empty (no collision).

**Step 6: Verify**

Confirm `data/tilesets/station.tres` appears in FileSystem with no import errors.

**Step 7: Commit**

```bash
git add data/tilesets/station.tres
git commit -m "feat: add station TileSet with Kenney atlas source and wall physics layer"
```

---

### Task 9: Write and run `tools/generate_rooms.gd`

**Files:**
- Create: `tools/generate_rooms.gd`
- Modify (generated): `scenes/areas/cantina.tscn`
- Modify (generated): `scenes/areas/engineering.tscn`
- Modify (generated): `scenes/areas/quarters.tscn`
- Modify (generated): `scenes/areas/derelict_entrance.tscn`

**Depends on:** Task 8
**Parallelizable with:** none (Task 10 depends on this script's generated output; no other tasks touch the same files)

**Step 1: Write `tools/generate_rooms.gd`**

```gdscript
# tools/generate_rooms.gd
# Run via Godot Script Editor: File → Run (or Ctrl+Shift+X)
@tool
extends EditorScript

const ROOM_W: int = 30
const ROOM_H: int = 16
const TILE_SOURCE_ID: int = 0
const TILESET_PATH: String = "res://data/tilesets/station.tres"

# ⚠ UPDATE THESE after importing your tileset in Task 8
# Open station.tres in the TileSet editor, hover tiles to read their atlas coords
const FLOOR_ATLAS: Vector2i = Vector2i(0, 0)  # <-- set to your floor tile
const WALL_ATLAS: Vector2i  = Vector2i(1, 0)  # <-- set to your wall tile

func _run() -> void:
	var tileset = load(TILESET_PATH)
	if not tileset:
		push_error("station.tres not found at: " + TILESET_PATH)
		return

	_process_area(
		"res://scenes/areas/cantina.tscn",
		tileset,
		{"right_door": true, "left_door": false},
		{
			"EngineeringDoor": Vector2(472, 128),
			"MarisSpawn":      Vector2(120, 128),
			"SableSpawn":      Vector2(240, 128),
			"RationsNode":     Vector2(80, 80),
		}
	)

	_process_area(
		"res://scenes/areas/engineering.tscn",
		tileset,
		{"right_door": true, "left_door": true},
		{
			"CantinaDoor":  Vector2(8, 128),
			"QuartersDoor": Vector2(472, 128),
			"DexSpawn":     Vector2(300, 128),
			"Workbench":    Vector2(240, 128),
			"PartsNode":    Vector2(80, 80),
			"EnergyNode":   Vector2(80, 176),
		}
	)

	_process_area(
		"res://scenes/areas/quarters.tscn",
		tileset,
		{"right_door": false, "left_door": true},
		{
			"EngineeringDoor": Vector2(8, 128),
			"PlayerBunk":      Vector2(240, 128),
		}
	)

	_process_area(
		"res://scenes/areas/derelict_entrance.tscn",
		tileset,
		{"right_door": false, "left_door": true},
		{
			"EngineeringDoor": Vector2(8, 128),
			"EntranceTrigger": Vector2(240, 128),
			"DoorVisual":      Vector2(240, 128),
			"DoorLabel":       Vector2(170, 100),
		}
	)

	print("✓ Room generation complete.")

func _process_area(
	scene_path: String,
	tileset: TileSet,
	doors: Dictionary,
	node_positions: Dictionary
) -> void:
	var packed: PackedScene = load(scene_path)
	var scene: Node = packed.instantiate()

	# Remove old Polygon2D floor and StaticBody2D walls
	for old_name in ["Floor", "Walls"]:
		var old: Node = scene.get_node_or_null(old_name)
		if old:
			scene.remove_child(old)
			old.free()

	# Remove existing TileMapLayer if regenerating
	var existing_tm: Node = scene.get_node_or_null("TileMapLayer")
	if existing_tm:
		scene.remove_child(existing_tm)
		existing_tm.free()

	# Create and configure TileMapLayer
	var tilemap: TileMapLayer = TileMapLayer.new()
	tilemap.name = "TileMapLayer"
	tilemap.tile_set = tileset
	scene.add_child(tilemap)
	tilemap.owner = scene
	scene.move_child(tilemap, 0)  # Render behind all other nodes

	# Paint tiles from layout
	var layout: Array = _make_layout(doors.get("right_door", false), doors.get("left_door", false))
	for y in ROOM_H:
		for x in ROOM_W:
			var atlas: Vector2i = WALL_ATLAS if layout[y][x] == 1 else FLOOR_ATLAS
			tilemap.set_cell(Vector2i(x, y), TILE_SOURCE_ID, atlas)

	# Update node positions (doors, spawns, props)
	for node_name in node_positions:
		var node: Node = scene.find_child(node_name, true, false)
		if node and node.has_method("set") and "position" in node:
			node.position = node_positions[node_name]
		elif node:
			push_warning("Node '%s' found but has no position property" % node_name)
		else:
			push_warning("Node '%s' not found in %s" % [node_name, scene_path])

	# Save scene
	var new_packed: PackedScene = PackedScene.new()
	new_packed.pack(scene)
	ResourceSaver.save(new_packed, scene_path)
	scene.free()
	print("  ✓ Generated: ", scene_path)

func _make_layout(right_door: bool, left_door: bool) -> Array:
	# Returns a ROOM_H × ROOM_W array: 1 = wall tile, 0 = floor tile
	# Door gaps: 3 tiles tall at y=6, 7, 8 on left/right walls
	var layout: Array = []
	for y in ROOM_H:
		var row: Array = []
		for x in ROOM_W:
			var is_wall: bool = false
			if y == 0 or y == ROOM_H - 1:
				is_wall = true
			elif x == 0:
				is_wall = not (left_door and y >= 6 and y <= 8)
			elif x == ROOM_W - 1:
				is_wall = not (right_door and y >= 6 and y <= 8)
			row.append(1 if is_wall else 0)
		layout.append(row)
	return layout
```

**Step 2: Set FLOOR_ATLAS and WALL_ATLAS constants**

Before running: open `tools/generate_rooms.gd`. Update `FLOOR_ATLAS` and `WALL_ATLAS` to match the atlas coordinates you identified in Task 8, Step 3.

**Step 3: Run the script**

In Godot, open `tools/generate_rooms.gd` in the Script editor.

Go to **File → Run** (or press `Ctrl+Shift+X`).

Expected output in the Godot console:
```
  ✓ Generated: res://scenes/areas/cantina.tscn
  ✓ Generated: res://scenes/areas/engineering.tscn
  ✓ Generated: res://scenes/areas/quarters.tscn
  ✓ Generated: res://scenes/areas/derelict_entrance.tscn
✓ Room generation complete.
```

If warnings appear about missing nodes, verify node names in the `.tscn` files match the `node_positions` dictionaries.

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add tools/generate_rooms.gd scenes/areas/
git commit -m "feat: generate TileMapLayer rooms for all 4 areas via EditorScript"
```

---

### Task 10: Update area scripts for new room dimensions

**Files:**
- Modify: `scripts/areas/cantina.gd`
- Modify: `scripts/areas/engineering.gd`
- Modify: `scripts/areas/quarters.gd`
- Modify: `scripts/areas/derelict_entrance.gd`

**Depends on:** Task 9
**Parallelizable with:** none (depends on Task 9 output to confirm node names)

Area scripts reference door nodes by name (`$EngineeringDoor` etc.). These node names are preserved by `generate_rooms.gd` — only positions changed. No script changes needed for door connections.

However, `cantina.gd` uses `get_tree().get_root().get_node("Main")` to call `go_to_area()`. Verify this still works after the main scene restructure. If the game path changed, update it.

**Step 1: Verify door scripts still work**

Run the game. Walk into a door trigger. Confirm the transition fires (fade + new room).

If no transition fires, open the relevant area script and check the door body_entered signal connection. The door Area2D's `CollisionShape2D` may need its shape size confirmed — it should be `RectangleShape2D(16, 48)` matching the 1×3 tile door gap. Verify in the Godot editor that the door Area2D collision shape overlaps the tile gap.

**Step 2: Update RationsNode parent in cantina (if needed)**

The `RationsNode` in cantina.tscn is a `Node2D` parent containing a `ResourceNode` instance. If `generate_rooms.gd` moved the parent to `Vector2(80, 80)`, verify the visual position looks correct in the editor.

**Step 3: Verify**

Run the game:
- Player cannot walk through walls (tile physics blocking)
- All 4 room transitions work (walk through door → fade → new room)
- NPCs spawn at correct positions in the new room size

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add scripts/areas/
git commit -m "fix: verify area scripts compatible with new 480x256 room layout"
```

## Batch 5 — UI Polish

### Task 11: Import m5x7 font as a reusable resource

**Files:**
- Create: `data/fonts/m5x7.tres`

**Depends on:** Task 2 (m5x7.ttf must be in `art/placeholder/fonts/`)
**Parallelizable with:** none (Tasks 12 and 13 depend on this)

**Step 1: Create font resource**

In Godot FileSystem, right-click `data/fonts/` → New Resource → `FontFile`. Save as `m5x7.tres`.

Select `data/fonts/m5x7.tres`. In the Inspector:
- `font_data`: set to `art/placeholder/fonts/m5x7.ttf`
- `antialiasing`: `None`
- `hinting`: `None`
- `subpixel_positioning`: `Disabled`
- `fixed_size`: `16` (m5x7 is designed for 16px — do not scale up with filtering)

**Step 2: Verify**

Create a temporary Label node anywhere in the editor. Set its font to `data/fonts/m5x7.tres` at size 16. Confirm the text looks crisp and pixel-perfect. Delete the temp node.

### Task 12: Redesign `dialogue_box.tscn`

**Files:**
- Modify: `scenes/ui/dialogue_box.tscn`
- Modify: `scripts/ui/dialogue_box.gd`

**Depends on:** Task 11
**Parallelizable with:** Task 13

**Step 1: Rebuild `dialogue_box.tscn`**

Open `scenes/ui/dialogue_box.tscn`. Rebuild the node tree to this structure:

```
CanvasLayer (DialogueBox)
  PanelContainer
    [Anchors: full-width bottom strip]
    [anchor_left=0, anchor_right=1, anchor_bottom=1, anchor_top=1]
    [offset_top=-64, offset_bottom=0]   ← 64px tall strip at bottom
    [StyleBoxFlat: bg_color=Color(0.08,0.08,0.12,0.95), border_width=1px, border_color=Color(0.4,0.4,0.6)]
    VBoxContainer
      HBoxContainer
        TextureRect (Portrait)
          [Name: Portrait]
          [custom_minimum_size: Vector2(32,32)]
          [stretch_mode: keep_aspect_centered]
        VBoxContainer
          Label (SpeakerLabel)
            [theme_override_fonts/font: data/fonts/m5x7.tres]
            [theme_override_font_sizes/font_size: 16]
          RichTextLabel (DialogueText)
            [theme_override_fonts/normal_font: data/fonts/m5x7.tres]
            [theme_override_font_sizes/normal_font_size: 16]
            [fit_content: true]
      VBoxContainer (ChoicesContainer)
        [theme_override_fonts/font: data/fonts/m5x7.tres for child Buttons]
```

For the `StyleBoxFlat` on `PanelContainer`: right-click the `Panel Style` property → New StyleBoxFlat. Set:
- `bg_color`: `Color(0.08, 0.08, 0.12, 0.95)`
- `border_width_left/right/top/bottom`: `1`
- `border_color`: `Color(0.4, 0.4, 0.6, 1)`
- `corner_radius_*`: `0` (pixel-perfect — no rounded corners)

**Step 2: Update `scripts/ui/dialogue_box.gd`**

Update node paths to match new structure:

```gdscript
# scripts/ui/dialogue_box.gd
extends CanvasLayer

@onready var speaker_label: Label = $PanelContainer/VBoxContainer/HBoxContainer/VBoxContainer/SpeakerLabel
@onready var dialogue_text: RichTextLabel = $PanelContainer/VBoxContainer/HBoxContainer/VBoxContainer/DialogueText
@onready var choices_container: VBoxContainer = $PanelContainer/VBoxContainer/ChoicesContainer
@onready var portrait: TextureRect = $PanelContainer/VBoxContainer/HBoxContainer/Portrait

func _ready() -> void:
	add_to_group("dialogue_box")
	hide()
	DialogueManager.conversation_ended.connect(hide)

func show_current_node() -> void:
	var node = DialogueManager.get_current_node()
	if node.is_empty():
		hide()
		return
	speaker_label.text = node.get("speaker", "")
	dialogue_text.text = node.get("text", "")
	_build_choices(node.get("choices", []))
	show()

func _build_choices(choices: Array) -> void:
	for child in choices_container.get_children():
		child.queue_free()

	if choices.is_empty():
		var btn = Button.new()
		btn.text = "..."
		btn.add_theme_font_override("font", load("res://data/fonts/m5x7.tres"))
		btn.add_theme_font_size_override("font_size", 16)
		btn.pressed.connect(hide)
		choices_container.add_child(btn)
		return

	var inner_voice_flags = DialogueManager.get_inner_voice_flags()
	for i in range(choices.size()):
		var choice = choices[i]
		var btn = Button.new()
		var label = choice.get("text", "")
		btn.add_theme_font_override("font", load("res://data/fonts/m5x7.tres"))
		btn.add_theme_font_size_override("font_size", 16)
		if inner_voice_flags.has(i):
			btn.text = "[%s] %s" % [GameState.player_background.capitalize(), label]
			btn.add_theme_color_override("font_color", Color(0.4, 0.8, 1.0))
		else:
			btn.text = label
		btn.pressed.connect(_on_choice.bind(i))
		choices_container.add_child(btn)

func _on_choice(index: int) -> void:
	DialogueManager.make_choice(index)
	if not DialogueManager.is_conversation_over():
		show_current_node()
```

**Step 3: Verify**

Run the game. Interact with an NPC. Confirm:
- Dialogue box appears at the bottom of screen (64px strip)
- Text uses m5x7 pixel font — crisp, no blur
- Speaker name shown above dialogue text
- Choice buttons readable

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add scenes/ui/dialogue_box.tscn scripts/ui/dialogue_box.gd
git commit -m "feat: dialogue box — 9-slice bottom panel, portrait slot, m5x7 font"
```

### Task 13: Apply m5x7 font to `hud.tscn` and `crafting_panel.tscn`

**Files:**
- Modify: `scenes/ui/hud.tscn`
- Modify: `scenes/ui/crafting_panel.tscn`

**Depends on:** Task 11
**Parallelizable with:** Task 12

**Step 1: Update `hud.tscn`**

Open `scenes/ui/hud.tscn`. For each `Label` node in the HUD:
- In the Inspector under "Theme Overrides > Fonts":
  - `font`: `data/fonts/m5x7.tres`
  - `font_size`: `16`

Confirm the HUD `CanvasLayer` layer is set to a value that keeps it above the game world (e.g. `layer = 1`). Align labels to a 16px grid if they are not already.

**Step 2: Update `crafting_panel.tscn`**

Open `scenes/ui/crafting_panel.tscn`. For each `Label` and `Button` node:
- Set font theme override: `data/fonts/m5x7.tres` at size `16`

For the `PanelContainer` background: add a `StyleBoxFlat` matching the dialogue box style:
- `bg_color`: `Color(0.08, 0.08, 0.12, 0.95)`
- `border_color`: `Color(0.4, 0.4, 0.6, 1)`

Note: dynamically created `Button` nodes in `crafting_panel.gd` do not inherit theme overrides automatically. Add the font override to the button creation code:

In `scripts/ui/crafting_panel.gd`, in `_build_recipe_list()`, after `var btn = Button.new()`:
```gdscript
btn.add_theme_font_override("font", load("res://data/fonts/m5x7.tres"))
btn.add_theme_font_size_override("font_size", 16)
```

**Step 3: Verify**

Run the game. Open the crafting panel. Confirm all text uses the m5x7 pixel font. HUD resource labels also in pixel font.

**Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add scenes/ui/hud.tscn scenes/ui/crafting_panel.tscn scripts/ui/crafting_panel.gd
git commit -m "feat: apply m5x7 pixel font to HUD and crafting panel"
```

---

#### Parallel Execution Groups — Smoketest Checkpoint 5

| Group | Tasks | Notes |
|-------|-------|-------|
| A (sequential) | Task 11 | Must complete before Tasks 12 and 13 — provides font resource |
| B (parallel) | Task 12, Task 13 | Different output files, no shared state |

### Smoketest Checkpoint 5 — Full playthrough with pixel-art UI

**Step 1: Fetch and merge latest master**
```bash
git fetch origin && git merge origin/master
```

**Step 2: Run all GUT tests**
```bash
godot --headless -s addons/gut/gut_cmdln.gd
```
Expected: All tests pass, zero failures.

**Step 3: Full playthrough**
```bash
godot
```

Walk through the complete loop:
1. Launch game — HUD visible with m5x7 pixel font
2. Walk player around Cantina — sprite animates, faces 4 directions
3. Interact with an NPC — dialogue box appears at screen bottom, m5x7 font, portrait slot visible
4. Open crafting panel — all text in m5x7 font
5. Walk through door to Engineering — fade to black, appear in Engineering with tiled walls
6. Walk through door to Quarters — same
7. Return to Engineering, then Cantina
8. Confirm player cannot walk through any wall tile
9. Confirm y-sort: walk behind the Workbench prop in Engineering, player renders behind it
10. Resize the window — game scales cleanly without blur at any window size

**Step 4: Confirm with user**
Tell the user: "Can you confirm the full loop works — tiled rooms, crisp pixel art, m5x7 font on all UI, fade transitions, wall collision, and y-sort?"

---

## Acceptance Criteria Checklist

| AC | Criteria | Task |
|----|----------|------|
| AC1 | `project.godot` sets 480×270, canvas_items, keep, Nearest | Task 1 |
| AC2 | Cantina, Engineering, Quarters, DerelictEntrance use TileMapLayer; fit viewport | Task 9 |
| AC3 | Player uses 16×32 AnimatedSprite2D with idle/walk for all 4 directions | Task 4 |
| AC4 | Player moves freely with diagonal support; facing direction updates from dominant axis | Task 4 |
| AC5 | Y-sort on AreaContainer; player behind prop renders behind it | Task 6 |
| AC6 | Wall tiles block movement; props blocked by StaticBody2D | Task 8+9 |
| AC7 | HUD, dialogue box, crafting panel use CanvasLayer + m5x7 font | Tasks 11–13 |
| AC8 | Room transition between any two areas plays fade-to-black | Task 7 |
| AC9 | Game renders crisp at 1080p (4×) and other integer scales | Task 1 |
